message:
feat(math): expand math ABI coverage with exp, float classification, and trig implementations

Extends the frankenlibc math subsystem with substantial new ABI surface area:

- math_abi.rs: add 600+ lines of ABI bindings for exp/log family (exp2, expm1,
  log2, log10, log1p), float classification (fpclassify, isinf, isnan, isnormal,
  signbit, copysign, fabs, fmod, remainder, fma), and trigonometric functions
  (sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2, sinh, cosh, tanh)
- libc.map: export 17 new math symbols in the GLIBC_2.2.5 version script
- exp.rs: add exp2/expm1/log2/log10/log1p implementations with proper edge-case
  handling (NaN propagation, infinity, zero, subnormal inputs)
- float.rs: add fpclassify/isinf/isnan/isnormal/signbit/copysign/fabs/fmod/
  remainder/fma with IEEE 754 compliant behavior
- trig.rs: add sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/atan2/sinh/cosh/tanh built on
  libm for correctness, with domain validation
- mod.rs: register new submodules in math module hierarchy
- support_matrix.json: update coverage matrix with 191+ new entries reflecting
  the expanded math ABI surface
- Update beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -243,7 +243,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2vv.3","title":"Symbol expansion: stdio family (phased approach)","description":"Complete stdio family coverage - the largest and most complex subsystem.

Current State:
- FILE* operations: mostly GlibcCallThrough
- printf family: partial Implemented (format engine exists)
- scanf family: likely stubs
- fopen/fclose/fread/fwrite: GlibcCallThrough

Target Architecture:
FrankenLibC needs its own FILE structure and buffering:
1. FILE state machine (read/write/append modes)
2. Buffer management (fully buffered, line buffered, unbuffered)
3. Error and EOF flag handling
4. Wide character support (fwide, fwprintf)

Implementation Phases:
Phase 1: Core FILE operations
- fopen, fclose, fflush
- fread, fwrite
- fgetc, fputc, fgets, fputs

Phase 2: Formatted I/O
- printf family (fprintf, sprintf, snprintf, asprintf)
- scanf family (fscanf, sscanf)

Phase 3: Positioning
- fseek, ftell, fgetpos, fsetpos
- rewind

Phase 4: Misc
- freopen, fdopen, fileno
- setvbuf, setbuf
- clearerr, feof, ferror

Testing:
- Unit: each FILE operation in isolation
- Integration: multi-step file processing workflows
- Concurrency: thread-safe FILE operations
- Hardened: handling of invalid FILE pointers

Complexity Warning:
This is ~20% of glibc by LOC. Needs phased approach.

Success Criteria:
- All stdio.h functions classified Implemented
- Zero glibc call-through for stdio
- Python hello world works (heavy stdio user)

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:17.632881169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:35.461593982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.3","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:48.526589618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:48.630694208Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:17.632881169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv.3.1","title":"Stdio Phase 1: FILE structure and basic I/O","description":"Stdio Phase 1: Core FILE structure and basic operations.

Goal: Implement FrankenLibC's own FILE structure with basic I/O.

FILE Structure Design:
- File descriptor (int fd)
- Buffer pointer and size
- Buffer position (read/write)
- Flags (read/write/append/binary)
- Error and EOF flags
- Lock for thread safety
- Wide orientation flag

Phase 1 Functions:
- fopen, fclose, fflush
- fread, fwrite
- fgetc, fputc, ungetc
- fgets, fputs
- fileno

Buffer Management:
- Full buffering (_IOFBF)
- Line buffering (_IOLBF)
- No buffering (_IONBF)
- setvbuf, setbuf

Standard Streams:
- stdin, stdout, stderr initialization
- Connection to fd 0, 1, 2

Testing:
- Unit: each operation in isolation
- Integration: read/write round-trips
- Concurrency: concurrent access to same FILE
- Error: invalid FILE pointer handling

Success Criteria:
- Basic file I/O works
- Buffer management correct
- Thread safety maintained

## Acceptance Criteria

","created_at":"2026-02-16T20:27:34Z"},{"id":427,"issue_id":"bd-2vv.3.1","author":"BeigePeak","text":"Progress update (BeigePeak, 2026-02-16): added deterministic stdio phase-1 fixture/e2e evidence lane and validated strict+hardened execution artifacts.

Implementation surfaces:

Fixture-e2e runs:

- Result: suite FAIL due unrelated existing fixtures; **`fixture_stdio` passed in both modes**.

2) Bead-scoped stdio strict+hardened run (deterministic closure evidence for this slice):

 {"id":"bd-2vv.3.2","title":"Stdio Phase 2: Printf family","description":"Stdio Phase 2: Printf family implementation.

Goal: Complete printf format engine.

Printf Functions:
- printf, fprintf, sprintf, snprintf
- vprintf, vfprintf, vsprintf, vsnprintf
- asprintf, vasprintf (GNU extensions)
- dprintf, vdprintf

Format Specifiers:
- Integer: d, i, u, o, x, X, b (C23)
- Float: f, F, e, E, g, G, a, A
- Character: c, s, p, n, %
- Width and precision: *, digit
- Flags: -, +, space, #, 0
- Length modifiers: hh, h, l, ll, j, z, t, L

Implementation Strategy:
Format engine in frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs:
1. Parse format string
2. Extract arguments from va_list
3. Format each specifier
4. Output to destination

Complexity Points:
- Floating-point formatting (Ryu or Dragonbox)
- Positional arguments ($)
- Wide character output (wprintf family)

Testing:
- Unit: each specifier in isolation
- Edge cases: width overflow, precision truncation
- Locale: decimal separator (depends on locale)
- Security: %n restrictions in hardened mode

Success Criteria:
- All specifiers implemented
- Output matches glibc format
- Hardened mode restricts %n

## Acceptance Criteria

Changes:

Status: keeping `bd-2vv.3.2` in progress for remaining printf-family surface (`v*printf`, `asprintf/vasprintf`, `vdprintf`).

-{"id":"bd-2vv.4","title":"Symbol expansion: math family with precision validation","description":"Complete math family coverage with correct rounding.

Current State:
- Basic functions (sin, cos, exp, log): likely Implemented via libm
- Special functions (gamma, bessel): likely stubs
- Float variants (sinf, sinl): partial
- FP environment (fenv): partial

Implementation Strategy:
Most math can delegate to Rust's libm crate or system libm.
Key is ensuring:
1. Correct rounding modes
2. NaN/Inf handling
3. errno setting (EDOM, ERANGE)
4. Exception flags (FE_DIVBYZERO, etc.)

Target Functions:
- Trig: sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2
- Hyperbolic: sinh, cosh, tanh, asinh, acosh, atanh
- Exponential: exp, exp2, expm1, log, log2, log10, log1p
- Power: pow, sqrt, cbrt, hypot
- Special: lgamma, tgamma, erf, erfc, j0, j1, jn, y0, y1, yn
- Rounding: ceil, floor, trunc, round, nearbyint, rint
- Remainder: fmod, remainder, remquo
- FP manipulation: frexp, ldexp, modf, scalbn, copysign
- Classification: fpclassify, isnan, isinf, isfinite, isnormal
- FP environment: fegetround, fesetround, feclearexcept, etc.

Testing:
- Unit: boundary values (±0, ±Inf, NaN, denormals)
- Precision: compare with mpfr for accuracy
- Rounding modes: test all four modes
- Errno: verify correct errno setting

Notes:
- float/double/long double variants (f, l suffixes)
- Some functions are macros in glibc (type-generic)

Success Criteria:
- All math.h functions classified Implemented
- Precision within 1 ULP of reference
- Correct errno/exception behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:27.175805833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:35.237756296Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","math"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.4","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:48.739149598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:48.851905294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:27.175805833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Current State:
- Basic functions (sin, cos, exp, log): likely Implemented via libm
- Special functions (gamma, bessel): likely stubs
- Float variants (sinf, sinl): partial
- FP environment (fenv): partial

Implementation Strategy:

3. errno setting (EDOM, ERANGE)
4. Exception flags (FE_DIVBYZERO, etc.)

Target Functions:
- Trig: sin, cos, tan, asin, acos, atan, atan2
- Hyperbolic: sinh, cosh, tanh, asinh, acosh, atanh
- Exponential: exp, exp2, expm1, log, log2, log10, log1p
- Power: pow, sqrt, cbrt, hypot
- Special: lgamma, tgamma, erf, erfc, j0, j1, jn, y0, y1, yn
- Rounding: ceil, floor, trunc, round, nearbyint, rint
- Remainder: fmod, remainder, remquo
- FP manipulation: frexp, ldexp, modf, scalbn, copysign
- Classification: fpclassify, isnan, isinf, isfinite, isnormal
- FP environment: fegetround, fesetround, feclearexcept, etc.

Testing:
- Unit: boundary values (±0, ±Inf, NaN, denormals)
- Precision: compare with mpfr for accuracy
- Rounding modes: test all four modes
- Errno: verify correct errno setting

Notes:
- float/double/long double variants (f, l suffixes)
- Some functions are macros in glibc (type-generic)

Success Criteria:
- All math.h functions classified Implemented
- Precision within 1 ULP of reference
- Correct errno/exception behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

 {"id":"bd-2vv.5","title":"Symbol expansion: socket/network family","description":"Complete socket/network family coverage.

Current State:
- Basic socket ops: likely RawSyscall
- Address functions (inet_pton, inet_ntop): partial Implemented
- Resolver (getaddrinfo, getnameinfo): GlibcCallThrough

Target Functions:
Socket operations (most are RawSyscall):
- socket, bind, listen, accept, connect
- send, sendto, sendmsg, recv, recvfrom, recvmsg
- shutdown, getsockname, getpeername
- setsockopt, getsockopt
- socketpair

Address conversion:
- inet_aton, inet_addr, inet_ntoa (legacy IPv4)
- inet_pton, inet_ntop (modern)
- htonl, htons, ntohl, ntohs (byte order)

Name resolution (requires NSS):
- getaddrinfo, freeaddrinfo, gai_strerror
- getnameinfo
- gethostbyname, gethostbyaddr (legacy)
- getservbyname, getservbyport

Implementation Notes:
- Socket syscalls are straightforward RawSyscall
- Address functions are pure computation (safe Rust)
- Resolver requires NSS infrastructure (dependency on bd-1rf)

Testing:
- Unit: address conversion edge cases
- Integration: simple client/server socket pairs
- E2E: curl-style HTTP request
- Hardened: invalid address buffer handling

Success Criteria:
- All socket.h/netinet/in.h functions classified
- Simple TCP/UDP programs work
- Resolver returns correct addresses

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.

- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:40.935379292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:35.011297352Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","socket"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.5","depends_on_id":"bd-25n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:48.963853366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2ez","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:49.071978207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vv.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:40.935379292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vv.6","title":"Symbol expansion: time family with timezone","description":"Complete time family coverage with timezone support.

Current State:
- Basic time (time, gettimeofday): likely RawSyscall
- Clock functions (clock_gettime): RawSyscall
- Conversion (localtime, gmtime): partial
- Formatting (strftime, strptime): partial
- Timer functions: likely stubs

Target Functions:
Time retrieval:
- time, gettimeofday, clock_gettime, clock_getres

Conversion:
- localtime, localtime_r, gmtime, gmtime_r
- mktime, timegm
- asctime, asctime_r, ctime, ctime_r

Formatting:
- strftime (complex - locale-dependent)
- strptime (parsing - complex)

Timers:
- timer_create, timer_settime, timer_gettime, timer_delete
- setitimer, getitimer

Sleep:
- sleep, usleep, nanosleep, clock_nanosleep

Timezone:
- tzset, tzname, timezone, daylight

Implementation Notes:
- Time syscalls are straightforward
- Conversion requires timezone database handling
- strftime is locale-dependent (needs locale infrastructure)
- Timers require signal integration

Complexity:
Timezone handling is surprisingly complex:
- TZ environment variable parsing
- /etc/localtime reading
- Leap second handling
- Historical timezone changes

Testing:
- Unit: conversion round-trips
- Unit: DST boundary handling
- Integration: strftime with various format strings
- E2E: date command equivalent

Success Criteria:
- All time.h functions classified Implemented
- Timezone conversions match glibc
- strftime output matches glibc format

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-2vv.7","title":"Symbol expansion: signal family with safety","description":"Complete signal family coverage with async-signal-safety.

Current State:
- signal, sigaction: likely partial
- sigprocmask: likely RawSyscall
- raise, kill: likely RawSyscall
- sigsetjmp/siglongjmp: requires setjmp (bd-2ry)

Target Functions:
Basic signal handling:
- signal, sigaction
- sigprocmask, pthread_sigmask
- sigemptyset, sigfillset, sigaddset, sigdelset, sigismember

Signal delivery:
- raise, kill, killpg
- sigqueue (with value)

Signal waiting:
- pause, sigsuspend, sigwait, sigwaitinfo, sigtimedwait

Stack:
- sigaltstack

Non-local jump:
- sigsetjmp, siglongjmp (depends on setjmp impl)

Implementation Notes:
Signal handling is security-critical:
- Async-signal-safety requirements (can't call malloc in handler)
- Signal frame layout is architecture-specific
- SA_SIGINFO vs traditional handler signatures

TSM Considerations:
- Signal handlers run in restricted context
- Membrane must be async-signal-safe
- Validation in signal context must be fast/simple

Testing:
- Unit: signal mask operations
- Integration: signal handler registration and delivery
- Concurrency: signals + threads interaction
- E2E: Ctrl-C handling, SIGTERM graceful shutdown

Success Criteria:
- All signal.h functions classified
- Signal handlers work correctly
- No deadlocks in signal + membrane interaction

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
